name: Porma Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse del Porma – circular por el pantano
description: Cycle a difficult 20.3-mile (32.7 km) route around Embalse del Porma, enjoying mountain views and abandoned villages.
region_intro: No traffic touring cycling routes around Embalse del Porma are situated in the province of León, Spain, characterized by an expansive reservoir set against a mountainous backdrop. The region features diverse terrain, including dense forests and elevated viewpoints, with glimpses of the Picos de Europa. The landscape offers varied cycling conditions, from lakeside paths to routes traversing abandoned villages and mountainous passes. Best no traffic touring cycling routes around Embalse del Porma The most popular no traffic touring cycling route is Views from Tolibia de Abajo – Abandoned Village of Utrero loop from Puebla de Lillo, a 26.8 miles (43.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 20 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the reservoir and passes through historical village remnants. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Puerto de las Señales – Forfogones Waterfall loop from Puebla de Lillo, a difficult 16.0 miles (25.7 km) path. This route features mountainous terrain and leads to a waterfall. Local touring cyclists also love the Boñar – Church of San Pedro de Boñar loop from Boñar, a 12.8 miles (20.6 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 13 minutes. Touring cycling around Embalse del Porma is defined by reservoir vistas, mountainous terrain, and historical abandoned villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes present a significant challenge. The routes in Embalse del Porma are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.75 stars from around 5 reviews. More than 70 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Embalse del Porma's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic touring cycling routes are available around Embalse del Porma?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;9 dedicated no-traffic touring cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Embalse del Porma. These routes offer a variety of distances and elevations, allowing you to explore the stunning landscapes without vehicle interference.

Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty can I expect on these routes?
A: The no-traffic touring cycling routes around Embalse del Porma are generally challenging. Most routes are rated as &lt;em&gt;difficult&lt;/em&gt;, featuring significant elevation gains and a mix of unpaved tracks and asphalted roads. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20500776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from Tolibia de Abajo – Abandoned Village of Utrero loop&lt;/a&gt; covers over 43 km with more than 1100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any family-friendly no-traffic cycling options around Embalse del Porma?
A: While many routes are rated as difficult, the region offers diverse terrain. For families, it's recommended to look for shorter, flatter sections or paths specifically designed for leisure. Always check the route details for elevation and surface type to ensure it's suitable for all family members.

Q: Can I bring my dog on these no-traffic cycling routes?
A: Many outdoor areas around Embalse del Porma are dog-friendly, especially on natural trails. However, specific rules may apply in certain protected areas or near attractions like the Museo de la Fauna Salvaje. Always keep your dog on a leash and ensure they are well-behaved, especially when encountering other cyclists or wildlife. Check local signage for any restrictions.

Q: Are there any circular no-traffic cycling routes?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Embalse del Porma are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31851593" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boñar – Church of San Pedro de Boñar loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40011652" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerulleda – Puerto de Vegarada loop&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient exploration without needing to retrace your steps.

Q: What interesting sights or attractions can I see along the no-traffic cycling routes?
A: The routes offer stunning natural beauty and unique historical sites. You can cycle past the immense reservoir with its transparent waters and mountainous backdrop, often with views of the Picos de Europa. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20500776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from Tolibia de Abajo – Abandoned Village of Utrero loop&lt;/a&gt;, pass through the poignant ruins of abandoned villages like Vegamián and Utreros, submerged during the reservoir's creation. There are also numerous 'miradores' (viewpoints) offering magnificent perspectives.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region's mountainous terrain means you can encounter natural features like waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14989942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Señales – Forfogones Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt; is a specific route that includes the beautiful Forfogones Waterfall, offering a refreshing stop during your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year to cycle around Embalse del Porma?
A: The best time to cycle around Embalse del Porma is typically during the spring and autumn months. These seasons offer milder temperatures and beautiful scenery, with spring bringing lush greenery and autumn showcasing vibrant fall colors. Summer can be pleasant, but be prepared for warmer weather, especially at midday. Winter can be challenging due to snow and colder conditions, particularly at higher elevations.

Q: Where can I park my car when accessing these no-traffic routes?
A: Parking is generally available in the towns and villages surrounding Embalse del Porma, such as Boñar, Puebla de Lillo, and Valdelugueros, which serve as common starting points for many routes. Look for designated parking areas or inquire locally for the most convenient spots to begin your ride.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Embalse del Porma?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;3.75 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from 6 ratings. Reviewers often praise the stunning natural beauty, the quietness of the no-traffic routes, and the unique experience of cycling past historical sites like the abandoned villages. The challenging terrain and rewarding views are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any options for food and drink near the no-traffic cycling routes?
A: Yes, the towns and villages surrounding Embalse del Porma, such as Boñar, Puebla de Lillo, and Valdelugueros, offer various options for food and drink. You'll find local restaurants, cafes, and pubs where you can refuel and relax after your ride. It's always a good idea to carry snacks and water, especially on longer routes, as services may be sparse between villages.
tips: Although this reservoir was officially renamed Juan Benet in 1994, after the death of the engineer in charge of its construction, it is still popularly known as the Porma reservoir or swamp, due to the river that supplies it, or even Vegamián, after one of the towns that were flooded after its construction.

This viewpoint not only offers views of the swamp, which after all can be observed from different points, but also has the Peña Utrero in the background, offering one more picture, even a postcard. La Peña Utrero receives its name from the town of Utrero, which was also partially destroyed with the construction of the reservoir.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Embalse del Porma circular route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult for touring cycling. It covers approximately 32.7 km with about 560 meters of elevation gain, requiring good to very good fitness. Some sections may even require you to dismount and walk your bike.

faqs.question: What type of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily consists of tracks ('pistas'), making a mountain bike (BTT) highly recommended. You'll also encounter some asphalted roads, particularly after Rucayo, leading towards Boñar. Be prepared for two or three small sections where you might need to walk your bike.

faqs.question: Are there any specific hazards or features to be aware of?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are three unlit tunnels on the asphalted road sections. It is essential to carry lights for your safety when passing through them. Also, some sections may require walking your bike due to the terrain.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Embalse del Porma circular route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy spectacular views of the Cantabrian Mountains and the Porma reservoir. Key highlights include the Juan Benet Reservoir and the View of Embalse del Porma and Peña Utrero. The route also passes through abandoned villages like Camposolillo and Utrero, offering a unique historical perspective.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Embalse del Porma circular route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, common starting points for routes around the reservoir include areas near Puebla de Lillo or Boñar. Look for designated parking in these towns or along access roads to the reservoir.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse del Porma circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail itself is generally accessible for dogs, but they should be kept on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near roads. Always ensure your dog is fit enough for the distance and terrain, and carry enough water for both of you.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during the spring, early summer, and autumn months when the weather is milder. Winters can be cold with snow, and peak summer might be too hot. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for this area?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle the Embalse del Porma circular route. The area is part of the Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre, but general access for cycling is free. Always respect local regulations and signage within the park.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of other paths. Approximately 18% of the route follows the 'Embalse del Porma' trail, and a small portion (1%) uses the 'camino Vozmediano' trail.

faqs.question: Are there places to get food, drinks, or find accommodation nearby?
faqs.answer: Yes, towns like Boñar, which the route connects to, offer options for refreshments and accommodation. The Museo de la Fauna Salvaje, located along the route after Rucayo, could also be a potential stop for a break.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, it's recommended to carry sufficient food and water. Lights are essential for the unlit tunnels. A mountain bike (BTT) is highly recommended, and basic repair tools are always a good idea. Don't forget sun protection and appropriate clothing for the weather.

waypoints.name: View of Embalse del Porma and Peña Utrero
